safest_plan.entry_basis: IVB entry zone $76–$80, two-stage scale ($80 first leg, $76 second) — zero_live 2026-04-27 and 2026-05-11 charts (fibs 0.382≈$80 / 0.5≈$78 / 0.618≈$75.8). Only valid on a pullback: by 2026-06-05 price had already run to ~$94, so this is a level to wait for, not a fill available now. Do NOT chase at current levels.

targets.basis: First potential TP / low-volume node (LVN), labelled ~$96 on all four charts (zero_live)
Second potential TP $107, labelled on the 2026-04-27 / 05-11 / 06-05 charts (zero_live)
Third potential TP $117, labelled on the 2026-04-27 and 06-05 charts (zero_live)

safest_plan.stop_basis: Close below the IVB value-low / $76 support (0.618 fib ~75.8) — Zero's 'valid stop for low leverage' (chart 2026-04-27). For larger size Zero states the stop must sit below the point-of-control / gap support (~$70–71); the drawn stop zone bottoms at 66.77 on the image.
safest_plan._stop_note: Image-vs-text correction: the 2026-04-27 text gave invalidation as 'close below gap support (~70–71.4)', but the charts label a tighter valid stop just below $76 for low-leverage size, with the deeper ~$71 / 66.77 stop only needed when sizing up.

stance_by_source.summary: The breakout long already played out (+24% from the point-of-control) and is up near the first target — take partial profit, move stop to break-even, do not chase a fresh entry here.
Buy the $76–$80 value zone (aggressive on a 0.382/0.5/0.618 retest ~$75–82), reclaim the $82–83 value-high and continue toward $96 / $107; invalid on a close back below $76.
Scale in long: first buy $80, second buy $76, targeting higher into earnings; stop below $76 for low leverage, or below the point-of-control / gap support ~$70–71 if sizing up.

conflicts: Direction is not contested — all three Zero calls are bullish on the same $76–$80 buy zone and the same $96 / $107 / $117 targets. The genuine conflict is timing: that buy zone is now stale. By the newest read (5 Jun) price had already run to ~$94, so buying now chases a move that is already up near its first target (~$96), giving poor reward versus risk.

Image vs text correction: the 27 Apr text gave invalidation as 'close below gap support (~70–71.4)', but the charts label a tighter valid stop just below the $76 value-low for low-leverage size, with the deeper point-of-control stop (~$71, zone floor 66.77) needed only if you increase size.
The 11 May interim target 'value-high ~82–83 reclaim' has since been exceeded; the standing chart targets are ~$96, $107 and $117.
plain_english: Every source here is bullish on Robinhood and points the same way — there is no real disagreement about direction. The plan was to buy between $76 and $80 and aim for roughly $96, then $107, with a safety exit just below about $76. The catch is timing: by the most recent update the stock had already jumped to around $94, so the cheap entry is gone and buying now means chasing a move that is nearly at its first profit target. If you are already in, take some profit and protect the rest; if you are not, wait for a pullback toward $76–$80 before buying rather than entering up here.
